topic.what: ECCFROG522PP a 522-bit prime field elliptic curve in short Weierstrass form
topic.how: Deterministic generation from public seed using BLAKE3-based pipeline with published search indices coefficient index=1294798 base point index=0
topic.why_matters: Addresses transparency concerns in existing curves like NIST P-521 by making parameter origin fully inspectable and reproducible from public artifacts rather than relying on authority trust

characteristics.what_it_measures: Prime field bit-length for security level
characteristics.vs_baseline: Similar security class as NIST P-521
characteristics.context: ~128-bit classical security level comparable to P-521

characteristics.what_it_measures: Prime order of base point subgroup
characteristics.context: Cofactor one eliminates small subgroup concerns

characteristics.vs_baseline: Checked for k ≤ 200 no reduction found
characteristics.evidence: Section 6.3 reports no pk≡1 mod N for k ≤ 200
characteristics.context: Lower bound only; full embedding degree not computed

characteristics.what_it_measures: Quadratic twist security against invalid curve attacks
characteristics.vs_baseline: Large proven prime factor in twist order
characteristics.evidence: Section 6.4 reports approximately 505-bit proven prime factor dividing twist order
Section 6.2 states CM sanity check passes small square-free tests up to 100k
characteristics.context: Bounded check; independent reviewers can extend

characteristics.what_itasures: Coefficient search position in deterministic procedure
characteristics.evidence: Section 4 and 5.1 specify coefficient index i=1294798
characteristics.context: Base point index j=0 already yielded full-order point

requires: SageMath or similar for independent validation

novelty: Deterministic BLAKE3-based curve generation from public seed
Published search indices enabling full end-to-end reproducibility
Explicit CM and anti-MOV sanity check documentation
open_problems: Extending CM sanity checks beyond 100k bound
Broader independent security review needed for adoption
Full embedding degree computation not performed
Extended validation of twist security claims
